title: Multi-Coding Agent Support
description: Add multi-coding agent support to SpecLedger CLI, enabling users to launch any coding agent (Claude Code, OpenCode, Copilot CLI, Codex) via sl code command with per-agent configurable arguments and environment variables. Includes multi-agent selection during sl new/init with symlink-based sharing on macOS/Linux and file copies on Windows.

title: Setup: Agent Infrastructure
description: WHY: Shared infrastructure needed before any agent-related features can be implemented.
design: Files: internal/agent/registry.go, internal/agent/platform.go. Create agent registry with 4 agents (Claude, OpenCode, Copilot CLI, Codex) including CLI commands and install instructions. Add platform detection for Windows vs Unix.

title: Create agent registry with 4 agent definitions
description: WHY: Need a central registry of supported coding agents with their CLI commands and install instructions.

items.item: Agent struct defined with all required fields
Registry contains Claude Code, OpenCode, Copilot CLI, Codex
Lookup function works with case-insensitive matching

design: File: internal/agent/registry.go. Define Agent struct with Name, Command, ConfigDir, InstallCommand fields. Create Registry with 4 agents: Claude Code (claude), OpenCode (opencode), Copilot CLI (github-copilot), Codex (codex). Provide Lookup(name) and All() functions.
acceptance_criteria: Agent registry returns correct info for all 4 agents. Lookup by name works case-insensitively. Install commands are accurate.

title: Add platform detection for Windows/Unix
description: WHY: Need to detect OS to handle symlinks vs file copies appropriately.

items.item: IsWindows() function returns correct value for current OS
SymlinkOrCopy() handles both symlink and copy cases

design: File: internal/agent/platform.go. Create IsWindows() function using runtime.GOOS. Create SupportsSymlinks() that returns true for non-Windows. Add SymlinkOrCopy(src, dst) that uses os.Symlink on Unix and copies on Windows.
acceptance_criteria: Platform detection correctly identifies Windows vs Unix. SymlinkOrCopy creates symlinks on macOS/Linux and copies on Windows.

title: Extend config schema for per-agent settings
description: WHY: Need config schema to support agent.default and agent.<name>.arguments/env keys.
design: File: pkg/cli/config/schema.go. Add schema keys for agent.default (string). Add dynamic key pattern for agent.<name>.arguments and agent.<name>.env. Extend existing AgentConfig if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Config schema accepts agent.default key. Config schema accepts agent.claude.arguments, agent.opencode.arguments, etc. Schema validates agent names against registry.

title: US1+US2: Core Agent Launch & Config (P1)
description: WHY: Core value proposition - developers need a unified way to launch any coding agent with proper configuration. This is the MVP.
design: Files: pkg/cli/commands/code.go (NEW), pkg/cli/launcher/launcher.go (EXTEND), pkg/cli/launcher/agents.go (NEW). Implement sl code [<agent>] command. Extend launcher to support multiple agents. Read per-agent config for arguments and env vars. Detect missing binaries and show install commands.

title: Implement sl code command with agent selection
description: WHY: Users need a unified CLI command to launch any coding agent.
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/code.go. Create new Cobra command 'code' with optional positional argument for agent name. Default to agent.default config value if no argument. Register command in CLI root.
acceptance_criteria: sl code launches default agent. sl code claude launches Claude Code. sl code opencode launches OpenCode. Missing argument shows help.

title: Extend launcher for multi-agent support
description: WHY: Existing launcher only supports Claude Code. Need to support all 4 agents.
design: File: pkg/cli/launcher/launcher.go. Extend LaunchAgent function to accept agent definition. Use agent.Command as executable. Pass agent-specific env vars from config. Maintain backward compatibility with existing Claude launch.
acceptance_criteria: Launcher can start any registered agent. Environment variables from config are passed to agent process. Process inherits stdout/stderr/stdin.

title: Add binary detection with install command error
description: WHY: Users need helpful error messages when agent binary is not installed.

items.item: Error format matches spec: Error: 'X' not found. Install: Y

design: File: pkg/cli/launcher/agents.go. Add CheckAgentInstalled(agent) function using exec.LookPath. Return error with install command if not found. Format: Error: '<cmd>' not found. Install: <install-command>
acceptance_criteria: Missing binary shows error with install command. Error message is actionable and copy-pasteable. Exit code is non-zero on error.

title: Read per-agent config for arguments
description: WHY: Users need to pass custom arguments to each agent via config.
design: File: pkg/cli/config/config.go. Add GetAgentArguments(agentName) function. Read from agent.<name>.arguments config key. Merge global and project config with project precedence. Return parsed argument slice.
acceptance_criteria: Arguments from agent.<name>.arguments are passed to agent. Project config overrides global config. Arguments are properly split respecting quotes.

title: Wire sl code command to launcher
description: WHY: Connect the command implementation to the launcher logic.

items.item: Config merged and passed to launcher

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/code.go. In Run handler: resolve agent from argument or default, check binary exists, get config, call launcher. Handle errors with user-friendly messages.
acceptance_criteria: sl code successfully launches agent with config. Error messages are clear and actionable. Command exits with proper codes.

title: US3+US4: Multi-Agent Setup & Shared Config (P2)
description: WHY: Users want to set up projects with multiple agents and share commands/skills across them.
design: Files: pkg/cli/tui/sl_new.go (EXTEND), pkg/cli/tui/sl_init.go (EXTEND), pkg/cli/playbooks/copy.go (EXTEND). Change single-select to multi-select for agents. Create .agent/commands and .agent/skills directories. Create symlinks on Unix, copies on Windows. Add --force flag handling.

title: Extend TUI for multi-agent selection
description: WHY: Users need to select multiple agents during sl new and sl init.

items.item: Agent selection uses checkboxes instead of radio

design: Files: pkg/cli/tui/sl_new.go, pkg/cli/tui/sl_init.go. Change agent selection from radio buttons to checkboxes. Display all 4 agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Copilot CLI, Codex). Store selected agents as comma-separated list in answers map.
acceptance_criteria: User can select multiple agents with Space to toggle. Enter confirms selection. At least one agent required unless None selected.

title: Create .agent directory structure
description: WHY: Shared .agent/ directory is the source of truth for commands and skills.
design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/copy.go. Add function to create .agent/commands and .agent/skills directories. Migrate existing .claude/commands content to .agent/commands if present. Handle --force flag for existing .agent/ directory.
acceptance_criteria: .agent/commands and .agent/skills created. Existing content migrated. --force flag required if .agent/ exists.

title: Create symlinks or copies to agent directories
description: WHY: Agent-specific directories need to point to shared .agent/ content.
design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/copy.go. For each selected agent, create .<agent>/commands symlink/copy pointing to ../.agent/commands. Use platform.SymlinkOrCopy for OS-specific behavior. Handle --force flag.
acceptance_criteria: Symlinks created on macOS/Linux. Copies created on Windows. All selected agents have commands and skills directories.

title: Store selected agents in constitution
description: WHY: Need to track which agents were selected for project context.

items.item: Selected Agents field added to constitution

design: File: pkg/cli/config/constitution.go (or equivalent). Add Selected Agents field to constitution. Format: - **Selected Agents**: claude, opencode, github-copilot. Update during sl new and sl init.
acceptance_criteria: Selected agents stored in constitution.md. Format is human-readable. Can be read back for context.

title: US5: Per-Agent Custom Arguments (P3)
description: WHY: Users want to pass arbitrary arguments to each agent via per-agent config keys for flexibility.
design: This is mostly implemented in P1 tasks. Verify agent.<name>.arguments and agent.<name>.env work correctly. Add any missing edge case handling. Test with multiple flags and special characters.
acceptance_criteria: All arguments in agent.<name>.arguments passed to agent. Environment variables injected correctly. Works for all 4 agents.

title: Polish: Cross-Cutting Concerns
description: WHY: Final polish and documentation after all user stories are implemented.
design: Update README.md with sl code usage. Add agent configuration examples. Validate quickstart.md scenarios work. Clean up any TODO comments.

title: Verify per-agent arguments work correctly
description: WHY: Ensure agent.<name>.arguments config works as specified.
design: Manual testing: Set agent.claude.arguments with multiple flags. Run sl code claude. Verify all arguments passed. Test with quotes and special characters.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple flags passed correctly. Quoted strings handled. Special characters preserved.

title: Verify per-agent env vars work correctly
description: WHY: Ensure agent.<name>.env config injects environment variables.
design: Manual testing: Set agent.claude.env.TEST_VAR=value. Run sl code claude. Verify env var is set in agent process.
acceptance_criteria: Environment variables from config injected into agent process. Multiple env vars work. Values with spaces handled.

title: Update documentation for sl code
description: WHY: Users need documentation on how to use the new sl code command.
design: Update README.md with sl code usage section. Add examples for launching agents, setting config, multi-agent setup. Reference quickstart.md for detailed guide.
acceptance_criteria: README has sl code section. Examples are accurate. Links to quickstart.md work.

title: Validate quickstart.md scenarios
description: WHY: Ensure all scenarios in quickstart.md work as documented.
design: Walk through each scenario in quickstart.md: launch default, launch specific, set config, multi-agent setup. Verify expected outcomes. Update quickstart.md if any discrepancies.
acceptance_criteria: All quickstart scenarios work. Error messages match documentation. Windows-specific instructions accurate.
